fix: test isolation via OLY_STATE_DIR + add 10 PTY e2e tests

  directory override, bypassing platform-specific env hacks
- Fix cli_errors.rs: add OLY_SOCKET_NAME to prevent accidental
  connection to user's real daemon via default named pipe

  XDG_STATE_HOME/HOME for reliable test isolation
- Add 10 new PTY e2e tests:
  - logs contain no CPR/DSR escape artifacts
  - multiple concurrent sessions are independent
  - session status transitions visible in oly ls
  - high-bandwidth output preserves log integrity
  - logs --keep-color preserves ANSI codes
  - logs --wait-for-prompt returns after command exits
  - stopped session logs persist on disk after eviction
  - foreground start streams full output
  - input to non-existent session fails gracefully
  - empty session list shows hint

  persist filter in escape sequence pipeline section

fix: strip DSR/CPR query sequences from PTY output sent to clients

Terminal queries like \x1b[6n (CPR) and \x1b[5n (DSR) in the child's
output were passing through EscapeFilter to attach clients. The client's
terminal would respond with its own CPR response, which crossterm
captured as keyboard input and forwarded to the child's stdin, causing
programs like F# Interactive to receive garbage input.

Add regex patterns to filter_cpr_chunk() that strip DSR/CPR query
sequences (\x1b[\d+n) and their bare ConPTY variants ([56]n) before
output reaches clients. Includes unit tests for single-chunk and
cross-chunk-split scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: filter CPR/DSR/OSC responses before writing to output.log

Terminal query responses (e.g. \x1b[1;1R) echoed by the PTY driver were
being written to output.log unfiltered, causing garbage like [1;1R to
appear in 'oly logs' output. Apply EscapeFilter before disk writes via
a new persist_filter field on SessionRuntime. Ring buffer and broadcast
remain unfiltered since attach clients apply their own per-client filter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Set platformAutomerge: false so Renovate merges PRs itself, only
during the configured 2–7am window.

commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

* Initial plan

Implement complete vendor identity lifecycle management:

Messages.Contracts:
- VendorUserInvitationResent - for resending pending invitations
- VendorUserInvitationRevoked - for revoking pending invitations

TenantManagement (commands, validators, handlers):

Fixes #3985. Remove unix driver; augment ansi driver to support configurable size detection (#4821)

* Initial plan

* Fixes #3985. Remove unix driver; augment ansi driver to optionally support polling size detection

- Delete UnixComponentFactory, IUnixInput, UnixInput, UnixInputProcessor, UnixOutput
- Move shared Unix infrastructure (UnixIOHelper, UnixRawModeHelper, UnixTerminalHelper, SuspendHelper, UnixClipboard) to Drivers/ root
- Remove Names.UNIX and UNIX driver registration from DriverRegistry
- Update GetDefaultDriver() to return 'ansi' on Unix/macOS instead of 'unix'
- Add SizeDetectionMode enum (Polling/AnsiQuery)
- Add Driver.SizeDetection ConfigurationProperty defaulting to Polling
- Update AnsiComponentFactory.CreateSizeMonitor() to use config-based selection
- Register SizeDetectionMode in SourceGenerationContext for AOT
- Remove Unix driver tests for deleted classes
- Update all references to Names.UNIX and UnixComponentFactory

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix size detection: AnsiQuery default, SizeMonitorImpl init fix, Polling uses NativeSizeQuery, update docs and tests

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: use IOException in CreateNativeSizeQuery catch block with trace logging

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: ANSI driver now detects terminal resizes correctly

Previously, the ANSI driver was stuck at 80×25 and did not respond to terminal resize events due to using SizeMonitorImpl, which only saw a cached size. This commit ensures AnsiSizeMonitor is always used for AnsiOutput, regardless of SizeDetectionMode, enabling correct resize detection via CSI 18t queries. Added Trace.Lifecycle instrumentation for event/trace observability. Updated and added comprehensive tests for monitor behavior and trace output. Also restored and cleaned up clipboard, IO, and Windows VT helper files. Documentation details the bug, fix, and verification steps. The ANSI driver now reliably tracks terminal size changes on all supported platforms.

* Move SuspendHelper.cs to UnixHelpers directory

SuspendHelper contains only Unix libc P/Invoke (killpg, uname) for SIGTSTP.
It belongs in UnixHelpers alongside the other Unix-specific helpers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract flush P/Invoke from AnsiTerminalHelper to platform helpers

Move tcdrain/fsync to UnixIOHelper.FlushStdout() and
FlushFileBuffers to WindowsVTOutputHelper.FlushStdout().
Add isatty to UnixIOHelper for Phase 3.
AnsiTerminalHelper is now a pure dispatcher with zero DllImport.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract IsAttachedToTerminal P/Invoke to platform helpers

Create WindowsConsoleHelper with GetStdHandle/GetConsoleMode.
Driver.IsAttachedToTerminal dispatches to WindowsConsoleHelper
and UnixIOHelper.IsTerminal; contains zero DllImport itself.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move NetWinVTConsole to WindowsHelpers directory

NetWinVTConsole uses different console mode flags than
WindowsVTInputHelper (more conservative), so it cannot be
deleted as a straight replacement. Move it to WindowsHelpers
to consolidate all Win32 P/Invoke in the correct directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Code cleanup

Reformat and reorganize code for readability

Refactored several files to improve code readability and maintainability by reformatting constructor initializations, Bash command invocations, and P/Invoke declarations. No functional changes were made; all updates are organizational and stylistic.

* Improve suspend/resume: add cooked mode restore and diagnostics

Restore terminal to cooked mode (stty sane) before sending SIGTSTP so the
shell can function while the process is stopped. Add diagnostic logging to
the suspend/resume lifecycle and fix the killpg P/Invoke declaration
(SetLastError, parameter name).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix termios struct layout mismatch on macOS

The Termios P/Invoke struct used uint (4 bytes) for tcflag_t/speed_t and
NCCS=32, which matches Linux (56 bytes total) but is wrong for macOS where
tcflag_t/speed_t are unsigned long (8 bytes) and NCCS=20 (72 bytes total).
This caused a buffer overflow in tcgetattr and corrupted terminal state on
restore.

Replace the struct with a raw IntPtr buffer (128 bytes) since we only need
to save and restore the blob opaquely — no individual fields are read.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and
auto-implement steps with a recursive fs.readdirSync('lua') call.
This keeps codebase context up to date automatically as new Lua
modules are added without requiring workflow changes.

Previously only impact < 4 triggered the label. Now type=other always
gets 'evaluation needed' regardless of impact score, matching the
intended behaviour that unclassified issues always need manual review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

commit.message:
Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test execution (#114)

* Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test support

Implement thread-local dispatch for ARM32 architecture, matching the
existing x86_64 and aarch64 implementations. This enables parallel test
execution on ARM32 Linux targets by replacing the global mutex approach
with per-thread function replacement routing.

Changes:
- Add target_arch = "arm" to all thread-local dispatch cfg gates across
  thread_local_registry.rs, internal.rs, injector.rs, common.rs, and
  thread_safety.rs
- Implement ARM32 dispatcher JIT code generation (ARM mode) with
  register save/restore for both integer (r0-r3) and VFP (d0-d7) regs
- Add ARM32 trampoline creation with support for both ARM and Thumb mode
  jump-back sequences
- Add PC-relative instruction fixup for ARM mode trampolines
- Add ARM32 branch patch generation matching existing patch_arm.rs format
  including Thumb alignment handling
- Add ARM32 JIT code for boolean return values (MOV r0, #imm; BX lr)
- Enable allocate_jit_memory for ARM32 (uses simple mmap fallback)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ARM32 dispatcher stack alignment and patch register clobbering

- Fix AAPCS stack alignment violation: PUSH {r0-r3, r4, lr} (24 bytes,
  8-aligned) instead of PUSH {r0-r3, lr} (20 bytes, misaligned).
  Misaligned SP before BLX caused SIGABRT on armv7.

- Use r12 (IP, scratch register) instead of r7 (callee-saved/frame pointer)
  for Thumb branch patch. r7 clobbering corrupted the caller's frame pointer,
  causing SIGSEGV on thumbv7. Uses MOVW/MOVT r12 encoding which is also
  alignment-independent.

- Use r12 instead of r9 (callee-saved) for ARM branch patch.

* Reduce ARM32 test stress and add diagnostics for CI

- Add RUST_BACKTRACE=full for ARM target CI runs
- Run ARM tests with --test-threads=1 to avoid resource exhaustion
  on 32-bit compat runners (armv7 was hitting thread spawn limits)
- Reduce stress test THREAD_COUNT from 20 to 4 on ARM32

* Fix ARM32 function overflow with dynamic 4-byte patch size

Use dynamic patch sizing for ARM32: 4-byte B.W (Thumb) or B (ARM) when
the dispatcher is within branch range (±16MB/±32MB), falling back to
12-byte MOVW/MOVT/BX when it is not. This eliminates the previous
12-byte patch overflow into adjacent small functions that caused
SIGSEGV on thumbv7.

Key changes:
- Add ARM32 to constrained JIT allocation (±16MB range) in common.rs
- Pre-allocate dispatcher, then choose patch size based on distance
- For Thumb 4-byte patches, prepend a Thumb-to-ARM stub (LDR.W/BX)
  since Thumb B.W cannot switch processor mode
- Encode Thumb B.W (T4) and ARM B (unconditional) for 4-byte patches
- Add -j 1 to CI cargo test for ARM targets to prevent resource
  exhaustion from parallel test binary execution

* Add diagnostics for thumbv7 SIGSEGV in test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread

Add eprintln diagnostics to:
- install_dispatcher_arm32: log addresses, distance, patch size
- generate_branch_patch_arm32: verify B.W encoding round-trips correctly
- test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread: log each step to identify crash point

Also add isolated test run CI step for ARM targets to determine if the
crash is caused by accumulated state from prior tests.

* Fix ARM32 Thumb overlapping patch SIGILL and instruction boundary bugs

- Use std::hint::black_box in test helper functions to ensure minimum
  4-byte code size on Thumb (prevents compiler from generating 2-byte
  functions that cause overlapping patches)
- Add calculate_thumb_copy_size() to scan Thumb instructions and find
  the first complete instruction boundary >= patch_size bytes, preventing
  the trampoline from splitting a 32-bit Thumb instruction
- Add check_arm32_patch_overlap() to detect overlapping patches at
  runtime with clear diagnostic messages (only checks active patches
  with ref_count > 0)
- Remove diagnostic eprintln statements and isolated test CI step

---------

Fixes #4809 - Kitty Keyboard Protocol Phase 2: Rich keyboard event model (#4816)

* Start Phase 2: Rich keyboard event model

Mark Phase 2 as in-progress in the kitty keyboard protocol plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Complete Phase 2: Rich keyboard event model with KeyUp, EventType, and ModifierKey

- Add KeyEventType enum (Press/Repeat/Release) and ModifierKey enum for standalone modifier identity
- Extend Key with EventType, ModifierKey, and IsModifierOnly properties (additive, non-breaking)
- Add KeyUp event pipeline: IInputProcessor → IDriver → IKeyboard → Application → View
- Enable kitty flag 2 (report event types) alongside flag 1 (disambiguate escape codes)
- Extract shared ApplyModifiersAndEventType helper in AnsiKeyboardParserPattern base class
- Update CsiKeyPattern and CsiCursorPattern regexes to accept kitty modifiers:event_type format
- Map kitty modifier key codepoints (57358-57451) to ModifierKey enum in KittyKeyboardPattern
- Rename KittyKeyboardPhase1Flags → KittyKeyboardRequestedFlags
- Update Keys scenario to display event type, modifier key identity, and KeyUp events
- Update keyboard.md and drivers.md documentation
- Add 44 new tests (KeyEventTypeTests + KittyKeyboardPhase2Tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix pipeline injection mode and add kitty protocol pipeline tests

Fix two bugs that broke InputInjectionMode.Pipeline:
1. InputProcessorImpl used DateTime.Now instead of _timeProvider.Now for
   stale escape detection, causing immediate release with VirtualTimeProvider
2. AnsiInputProcessor.InjectKeyDownEvent/InjectMouseEvent routed through
   the background input thread; now enqueues directly to InputQueue

Simplify InputInjector.ProcessQueue() by removing the VirtualTimeProvider
60ms-advance workaround (no longer needed with bug #1 fixed).

Un-skip two Pipeline tests and add 27 kitty protocol pipeline tests
covering event types, modifiers, functional keys, standalone modifier
keys, CSI ~/cursor variants, full press-repeat-release cycles, and
negative cases (invalid event types, malformed sequences, recovery).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fixes #4582 - Windows console input encoding for accented chars

Previously, input bytes from the Windows console were always decoded as UTF-8, but ReadFile returns bytes in the console's input code page (e.g., Windows-1252). This caused accented and non-ASCII characters to be misread. This commit introduces a ConsoleInputEncoding property in WindowsVTInputHelper to dynamically use the correct encoding, and updates AnsiInput to use it. Adds unit tests to verify correct decoding of accented characters. Also includes minor test cleanups and improved naming.

* fixed test name

* Fixes link bug that was causing Windows tests to hang.

* Add support for standalone modifier key events (kitty)

Include kitty flag 8 to enable reporting of standalone modifier key events (e.g., Shift, Ctrl, Alt) as escape codes. Update tests to verify correct handling of modifier-only key press and release events, and assert that the requested kitty flags include "report all keys as escape codes." This improves compatibility with terminals supporting the kitty keyboard protocol.

* Update AI agent instructions with comprehensive Copilot guide and new coding conventions

- Replaced .github/copilot-instructions.md stub with full reference covering
  build/test commands, architecture overview, code style, terminology, and
  testing conventions
- Added early return/guard clause convention across all AI instruction files
- Added one-type-per-file convention for public and internal types
- Updated CONTRIBUTING.md (source of truth), CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: use KittyKeyboardFlags enum for protocol flags

Replaces raw int usage with strongly-typed KittyKeyboardFlags enum for kitty keyboard protocol flags throughout the codebase. Adds KittyKeyboardFlags.cs and moves KittyKeyboardProtocolResult to its own file, updating it to use the new enum. Updates all related logic, methods, and tests for improved type safety, clarity, and maintainability.

* Kitty protocol: add alternate key reporting (flag 4)

Adds full support for kitty keyboard protocol "alternate key reporting" (flag 4) in Terminal.Gui. The Key model now exposes ShiftedKeyCode and BaseLayoutKeyCode, populated by the kitty parser for each key event. Updates the parser, documentation, and tests to cover alternate key fields, international layouts, and end-to-end propagation. Replaces old phase 2 tests with new, comprehensive suites for parsing, pipeline, and Key object behavior. Improves operator and parsing logic in Key, and updates docs to guide usage of new features.

* Fix race condition in ResetState Driver disposal

Two threads (test + AppTestHelper background) could race into Dispose() →
ResetState() concurrently. Thread A nulls Driver between Thread B's null-check
and Driver.Dispose(), causing NullReferenceException.

Fix: capture Driver to local, null the property, then unsubscribe and dispose
using the captured local. Concurrent callers see null and skip cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
